moveAnnotations.annotation: We start with the English Opening, one of the most flexible and strategically rich first moves in chess. Rather than immediately grabbing the center with a pawn like 1.e4, we control the d5 square and prepare to build our center more gradually, keeping maximum flexibility in our setup. This move suits players who enjoy positional maneuvering and want to avoid the sharpest theoretical lines — we can transpose into many different systems depending on Black's response, whether they play 1...c5 (mirroring our structure), 1...e5, or other moves.
